description: This programme/post is labelled as a “pilot rotation”. This means that this programme has been created as part of a commitment to reduce the impact of rotations on Resident Doctors. In 2024, the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) and the British Medical Association (BMA) agreed to a jointly-led review of rotational training and in Autumn 2025 the NHS England 10 Point Plan for Resident Doctors committed to launching pilots with reformed rotational changes for August 2026. This programme/post is one of these pilots.All pilot posts meet standard curriculum requirements and do not affect training progression. If you preference one of these posts, you may be asked to provide feedback on your experience as part of the evaluation process. Participation in the evaluation is not mandatory. More information can be found in the News Bulletin and FAQ sections of Oriel.IM1 and IM2 years each consist of 3 x 4 month rotations at Frimley Park Hospital.IMT1 - Haematology/ITU , Respiratory/MADU, Geriatric MedicineIMT2 -Rheumatology, Gastroenterology, Acute Internal Medicine K79=FRIM11Pilot rotation

preference: London - North London - North Central East London - IMT - L109=NC10.2A
programme_name: Internal Medicine Training Stage 1 North East & Central LDN
description: This programme/post is labelled as a “pilot rotation”. This means that this programme has been created as part of a commitment to reduce the impact of rotations on Resident Doctors. In 2024, the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) and the British Medical Association (BMA) agreed to a jointly-led review of rotational training and in Autumn 2025 the NHS England 10 Point Plan for Resident Doctors (https://www.england.nhs.uk/long-read/10-point-plan-to-improve-resident-doctors-working-lives/) committed to launching pilots with reformed rotational changes for August 2026. This programme/post is one of these pilots. All pilot posts meet standard curriculum requirements and do not affect training progression. If you preference one of these posts, you may be asked to provide feedback on your experience as part of the evaluation process. Participation in the evaluation is not mandatory. More information can be found in the News Bulletin and FAQ sections of Oriel.Each rotation is for 6 months.IM11. Royal Free London NHS Foundation Trust, North Middlesex Hospital: Geriatric Medicine2. Royal Free London NHS Foundation Trust, North Middlesex Hospital: Gastroenterology*Please note these rotations may not be in this order*IM21. Royal Free London NHS Foundation Trust, North Middlesex Hospital: Acute Medicine/Intensive Care Medicine2. Royal Free London NHS Foundation Trust, North Middlesex Hospital: Haematology*Please note these rotations may not be in this order* L109=NC10.2APilot rotation

programme_name: Internal Medicine Training Stage 1 North East & Central LDN
description: This programme/post is labelled as a “pilot rotation”. This means that this programme has been created as part of a commitment to reduce the impact of rotations on Resident Doctors. In 2024, the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) and the British Medical Association (BMA) agreed to a jointly-led review of rotational training and in Autumn 2025 the NHS England 10 Point Plan for Resident Doctors (https://www.england.nhs.uk/long-read/10-point-plan-to-improve-resident-doctors-working-lives/) committed to launching pilots with reformed rotational changes for August 2026. This programme/post is one of these pilots. All pilot posts meet standard curriculum requirements and do not affect training progression. If you preference one of these posts, you may be asked to provide feedback on your experience as part of the evaluation process. Participation in the evaluation is not mandatory. More information can be found in the News Bulletin and FAQ sections of Oriel.Each rotation is for 6 months.IM11. description: This programme/post is labelled as a “pilot rotation”. This means that this programme has been created as part of a commitment to reduce the impact of rotations on Resident Doctors. In 2024, the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) and the British Medical Association (BMA) agreed to a jointly-led review of rotational training and in Autumn 2025 the NHS England 10 Point Plan for Resident Doctors (https://www.england.nhs.uk/long-read/10-point-plan-to-improve-resident-doctors-working-lives/) committed to launching pilots with reformed rotational changes for August 2026. This programme/post is one of these pilots. All pilot posts meet standard curriculum requirements and do not affect training progression. If you preference one of these posts, you may be asked to provide feedback on your experience as part of the evaluation process. Participation in the evaluation is not mandatory. More information can be found in the News Bulletin and FAQ sections of Oriel.IMT Year 1:London North West University Healthcare NHS Trust  IMT Year 2: London North West University Healthcare NHS Trust Placements rotating between: [IMT1] -  Aug-26: Northwick Park Hospital - Intensive Care Medicine, Nov-26: Ealing Hospital: Acute Internal Medicine, Feb-27:Ealing Hospital - Geriatrics[IMT2] - Aug-27: Northwick Park Hospital - Gastroenterology, Feb-28:Northwick Park Hospital - Haematology/Acute internal MedicineIf you rank this post and apply for IMT3, your IMT3 allocation will be at one of these posts:Ealing Hospital – Acute internal MedicineEaling Hospital - Respiratory MedicineEaling Hospital - Geriatric MedicineL83=NW22APilot rotation

programme_name: Internal Medicine Training Stage 1 North West LDN
description: This programme/post is labelled as a “pilot rotation”. This means that this programme has been created as part of a commitment to reduce the impact of rotations on Resident Doctors. In 2024, the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) and the British Medical Association (BMA) agreed to a jointly-led review of rotational training and in Autumn 2025 the NHS England 10 Point Plan for Resident Doctors (https://www.england.nhs.uk/long-read/10-point-plan-to-improve-resident-doctors-working-lives/) committed to launching pilots with reformed rotational changes for August 2026. This programme/post is one of these pilots. All pilot posts meet standard curriculum requirements and do not affect training progression. If you preference one of these posts, you may be asked to provide feedback on your experience as part of the evaluation process. Participation in the evaluation is not mandatory. More information can be found in the News Bulletin and FAQ sections of Oriel.IMT Year 1:London North West University Healthcare NHS Trust  IMT Year 2: London North West University Healthcare NHS Trust Placements rotating between: [IMT1] -  Aug-26: Northwick Park Hospital - Respiratory Medicine, Feb-27:Ealing Hospital - Acute Internal Medicine, May-27:Northwick Park Hospital: Intensive Care Medicine[IMT2] - Aug-27: Northwick Park Hospital - Rheumatology, Feb-28:Northwick Park Hospital - Geriatric MedicineIf you rank this post and apply for IMT3, your IMT3 allocation will be at one of these posts:Ealing Hospital – Acute internal MedicineEaling Hospital - Respiratory MedicineEaling Hospital - Geriatric MedicineL86=NW22DPilot rotation

description: This programme/post is labelled as a “pilot rotation”. This means that this programme has been created as part of a commitment to reduce the impact of rotations on Resident Doctors. In 2024, the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) and the British Medical Association (BMA) agreed to a jointly-led review of rotational training and in Autumn 2025 the NHS England 10 Point Plan for Resident Doctors (https://www.england.nhs.uk/long-read/10-point-plan-to-improve-resident-doctors-working-lives/) committed to launching pilots with reformed rotational changes for August 2026. This programme/post is one of these pilots. All pilot posts meet standard curriculum requirements and do not affect training progression. If you preference one of these posts, you may be asked to provide feedback on your experience as part of the evaluation process. Participation in the evaluation is not mandatory. More information can be found in the News Bulletin and FAQ sections of Oriel.Each rotation is for 4 months.IM11. Lewisham and Greenwich NHS Trust, University Hospital Lewisham: Acute Medicine2. preference: London - South London - IMT - L275=SW18A
programme_name: Internal Medicine Training Stage 1 South West LDN
description: This programme/post is labelled as a “pilot rotation”. This means that this programme has been created as part of a commitment to reduce the impact of rotations on Resident Doctors. In 2024, the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) and the British Medical Association (BMA) agreed to a jointly-led review of rotational training and in Autumn 2025 the NHS England 10 Point Plan for Resident Doctors (https://www.england.nhs.uk/long-read/10-point-plan-to-improve-resident-doctors-working-lives/) committed to launching pilots with reformed rotational changes for August 2026. This programme/post is one of these pilots. All pilot posts meet standard curriculum requirements and do not affect training progression. If you preference one of these posts, you may be asked to provide feedback on your experience as part of the evaluation process. Participation in the evaluation is not mandatory. More information can be found in the News Bulletin and FAQ sections of Oriel.Each rotation is for 4 months.IM11. Croydon Health Service NHS Trust, Croydon University Hospital: Acute Medicine2.
